title: lappula physacantha
intro: Lappula physacantha, also known as the sticklepod or needle-fruit, is a plant species belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, Lappula physacantha is used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat infections and reduce swelling. The plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, relieving respiratory problems, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Lappula physacantha are often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
